short_name: Condensation of a digraph defines a partial order
novelty_status: CLASSICAL; ATTRIBUTE, DO NOT CLAIM NOVELTY
allowed_claim: A directed relation canonically induces an internal quotient order through SCC condensation.
forbidden_overclaim: Discovery of the SCC condensation theorem, physical time, or a unique temporal arrow.
editorial_note: Define SCC condensation only; do not motivate with physical time.

short_name: Full reachability preorder encodes SCC partition and quotient order
allowed_claim: The full labeled reachability preorder is an injective representation of the complete labeled condensation structure.
forbidden_overclaim: That the representation is a newly discovered graph invariant.
editorial_note: Use as the bridge between digraphs and complete labeled preorders.

short_name: Count of full labeled condensation preorders
novelty_status: CLASSICAL ENUMERATION FORMULA; PROJECT CONTRIBUTION IS INDEPENDENT REPRODUCTION AND CORRECTIVE APPLICATION
allowed_claim: The classical formula is independently rederived and exhaustively reproduced at n=5, and it is used to diagnose the earlier 5,234 encoding ambiguity.
forbidden_overclaim: A new formula for labeled preorders, a new value 6,942, or discovery of the preorder/topology correspondence.
editorial_note: State explicitly that the formula and 6,942 are classical.

novelty_status: PROJECT-SPECIFIC ELEMENTARY ENUMERATION; NO EXACT MATCH FOUND IN C1 SEARCH; PRIORITY NOT CERTIFIED
allowed_claim: The project defines a minimum-representative quotient-poset code and derives its exact shifted-binomial count.
forbidden_overclaim: A definitively first or deep general enumeration theorem.
editorial_note: Present as an elementary project-specific encoding count.

allowed_claim: The earlier wording was ambiguous because the implementation discarded SCC membership information.
forbidden_overclaim: Calling the discrepancy fabrication or treating all A8 outputs as invalid.
editorial_note: Use the explicit information-loss mechanism as the correction.

short_name: Three distinct equivalence levels at n=5
section: 5. Exact n=5 Structure and Fiber Data
allowed_claim: These exact finite counts distinguish three well-defined quotient levels.
forbidden_overclaim: An asymptotic result or a physical prediction.
editorial_note: Separate full, representative, and unlabeled equivalence levels.

short_name: Single-edge flip changes the n=5 condensation preorder with probability 75/256
novelty_status: APPARENTLY UNREPORTED EXACT FINITE SENSITIVITY STATISTIC; NOVELTY NOT CERTIFIED
allowed_claim: For the explicitly defined uniform graph-edge ensemble, exhaustive enumeration yields P2=1, P3=3/4, P4=1/2, and P5=75/256.
forbidden_overclaim: A general law, asymptotic theorem, universal constant, or categorical first-ever claim.
editorial_note: Define P_n before giving P_2,...,P_5; do not imply a general law.

short_name: Degree data do not determine the audited structural profile
allowed_claim: Degree information is insufficient for the particular structural observables audited at n=5.
forbidden_overclaim: A general asymptotic theorem, or a claim that degree data never identify any relevant structure.
editorial_note: Include only if it supports a broader encoding-information argument.
